root_text.sn48.41:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati pubbārāme migāramātupāsāde.
root_text.sn48.41:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito pacchātape nisinno hoti piṭṭhiṁ otāpayamāno.
root_text.sn48.41:2.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā bhagavato gattāni pāṇinā anomajjanto bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn48.41:2.3: Na cevaṁ dāni, bhante, bhagavato tāva parisuddho chavivaṇṇo pariyodāto, sithilāni ca gattāni sabbāni valiyajātāni, purato pabbhāro ca kāyo, dissati ca indriyānaṁ aññathattaṁ—
root_text.sn48.41:3.2: jarādhammo yobbaññe, byādhidhammo ārogye, maraṇadhammo jīvite.
root_text.sn48.41:3.3: Na ceva tāva parisuddho hoti chavivaṇṇo pariyodāto, sithilāni ca honti gattāni sabbāni valiyajātāni, purato pabbhāro ca kāyo, dissati ca indriyānaṁ aññathattaṁ—
root_text.sn48.41:4.2: Idaṁ vatvā ca sugato athāparaṁ etadavoca satthā:

translation_text.sn48.41:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in the stilt longhouse of Migāra’s mother in the Eastern Monastery.
translation_text.sn48.41:1.3: Then in the late afternoon, the Buddha came out of retreat and sat warming his back in the last rays of the sun.
translation_text.sn48.41:2.1: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, and while massaging the Buddha’s limbs he said:
translation_text.sn48.41:2.3: how the complexion of your skin is no longer pure and bright. Your limbs are flaccid and wrinkled, and your body is stooped. And it’s apparent that there has been a deterioration in your faculties
translation_text.sn48.41:2.4: of eye, ear, nose, tongue, and body.”
translation_text.sn48.41:3.2: When young you’re liable to grow old; when healthy you’re liable to get sick; and when alive you’re liable to die.
translation_text.sn48.41:3.3: The complexion of the skin is no longer pure and bright. The limbs are flaccid and wrinkled, and the body is stooped. And it’s apparent that there has been a deterioration in the faculties
translation_text.sn48.41:3.4: of eye, ear, nose, tongue, and body.”
translation_text.sn48.41:4.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn48.41:4.2: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn48.41:5.3: That’s how much this delightful puppet
translation_text.sn48.41:5.4: is ground down by old age.
translation_text.sn48.41:6.1: Even if you live for a hundred years,

variant_text.sn48.41:6.2: sopi maccuparāyaṇo → sabbe maccuparāyanā (sya-all, km, mr)
